hypothesis: If NEB calculations for LJ7 and LJ38 transitions are executed via NN-BAX that iteratively fine-tunes EquiformerV2 and acquires training points from model-predicted paths, then the method will reach ground-truth MEPs (meeting fmax and low MAEi) with fewer expensive simulator evaluations than classical NEB because the learned model yields accurate energies/forces and BAX targets the most informative regions of the path.
